name: Warren Vaché
abstract: Warren Vaché Jr. (born February 21, 1951) is an American jazz trumpeter, cornetist, and flugelhornist.

about_quotes.speaker: Vic Dickenson
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee (Vic Dickenson) does not speak in the provided context. The mention of Warren Vache is made solely by the Interviewer.

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Allen
about_quotes.evidence: I had met Scott Hamilton and Warren Vache and Major Holly and some people like that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Harry Allen interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 13, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Ken Peplowski
about_quotes.evidence: And there’s a friend of mine, Warren Vache, who said it’s not the traveling
about_quotes.doc_title: Ken Peplowski interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 10, 1997 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Flanigan
about_quotes.evidence: And I did one thing with Warren Vache... we were supposed to be the pit band... we arranged them and played.
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Flanigan interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 15, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Carmen Leggio
about_quotes.evidence: on the octet was Warren Vache, Urbie Green, Bucky Pizzarelli and Hank Jones, Slam Stewart and Benny.
about_quotes.doc_title: Carmen Leggio interviewed by Monk Rowe, Tarrytown, New York, October 31, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Howard Alden
about_quotes.evidence: Warren Vache, back in New York I play with a fair amount, again, usually like trios or quartets.
about_quotes.doc_title: Howard Alden interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997
